summary: Implemented the Phase 0B local-only single-admin gate without new dependencies, database changes, public deployment assumptions, or external auth providers. The slice adds an Edge-compatible auth helper, login/logout routes, login page, middleware protection for admin UI and admin APIs, local-only env/docs updates, home navigation into the protected workspace, and focused auth tests.

tests_run.result: PASS: 9 local admin auth tests passed.
PASS: Next.js production build passed; known local Windows SWC optional-native warnings were nonfatal.

decisions: Kept auth local-only and dependency-free with WebCrypto HMAC/session helpers that can run in middleware.
Rejected blank or checked-in placeholder admin password/session secret values so the committed example fails closed until .env.local is customized.
Protected both UI routes and admin data/action APIs; unauthenticated UI requests redirect to /login while API requests return sanitized 401 JSON.
Used httpOnly SameSite=Strict session cookie defaults, secure only in production, 12-hour max age, and token payload containing only local-admin subject plus timestamps.
Documented that this gate is not production auth or ownership and public deployment/provider/CI decisions remain human-gated.
risks: The local admin gate is intentionally single-admin and not a production multi-user auth/ownership model.
Developers must replace the checked-in ADMIN_PASSWORD and SESSION_SECRET placeholders in .env.local before manual use.
Windows Next build still prints known nonfatal optional SWC warnings.
non_goal_confirmation: No production auth provider, OAuth, signup, account management, ownership model, real secrets, external account, paid provider, public deployment, DNS, CI/CD, billing, telemetry, cron, email, package dependency, package-lock, Prisma schema, migration, seed, scan orchestration, scoring, provider, LLM, or Radar Task UI CRUD implementation was added.
requested_action: Review the local admin gate implementation for session/cookie safety, placeholder fail-closed behavior, protected UI/API coverage, secret leakage, and local-only documentation boundaries.
